Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Complex The Outer Space campaign aims to disrupt healthcare services by compromising healthcare organizations' systems and stealing sensitive data.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets User Account Network Traffic File System Network Share Script File
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Email Delivery Method PowerShell Script Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism) File Access (Mechanism) Network Share Access (Mechanism) Web Shell Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker gathers information about healthcare and public health sector targets using artificial intelligence.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attacker creates custom tools for the Outer Space campaign.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2023-2033 (Suggested): Google Chromium V8 Engine contains a type confusion vulnerability that allows a remote attacker to potentially exploit heap corruption via a crafted HTML page.
stage.description: The attacker uses compromised email accounts to send targeted phishing emails to healthcare personnel.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2017-9248 (Suggested): Progress Telerik UI for ASP.NET AJAX and Sitefinity have a cryptographic weakness in Telerik.Web.UI.dll that can be exploited to disclose encryption keys (Telerik.Web.UI.DialogParametersEncryptionKey and/or the MachineKey), perform cross-site-scripting (XSS) attacks, compromise the ASP.NET ViewState, and/or upload and download files.
stage.description: The attacker compromises healthcare websites using drive-by downloads.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2021-1675 (Suggested): Microsoft Windows Print Spooler contains an unspecified vulnerability that allows for remote code execution.
stage.capec: CAPEC-108: Command Line Execution through SQL Injection
stage.description: The attacker uses PowerShell to execute malicious scripts on compromised systems.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2023-36884 (Suggested): Microsoft Windows Search contains an unspecified vulnerability that could allow an attacker to evade Mark of the Web (MOTW) defenses via a specially crafted malicious file, leading to remote code execution.
stage.description: The attacker uses WMI to execute malicious code on compromised systems.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attacker schedules tasks to maintain persistence on compromised systems.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attacker installs bootkits to maintain persistence at the firmware level.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker exploits vulnerabilities in public-facing applications to gain elevated privileges.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker uses elevation control mechanisms to escalate privileges.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC-191: Read Sensitive Constants Within an Executable
stage.description: The attacker obfuscates malware code to evade detection.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.technique: T1198: SIP and Trust Provider Hijacking
stage.description: The attacker tampers with anti-virus software to evade detection.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attacker uses brute-force attacks to obtain credentials.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attacker uses keyloggers to capture login credentials.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attacker sniffs network traffic to gather information about healthcare networks.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attacker discovers system information to identify potential targets.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-645: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket)
stage.description: The attacker uses valid accounts to move laterally within the network.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attacker uses remote services to move laterally within the network.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attacker collects data from local systems.

stage.name: Collection
stage.technique: T1039: Data from Network Shared Drive
stage.capec: CAPEC-643: Identify Shared Files/Directories on System
stage.description: The attacker collects data from network shared drives.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attacker uses FTP to exfiltrate stolen data.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.capec: CAPEC-650: Upload a Web Shell to a Web Server
stage.description: The attacker uses web shells to exfiltrate stolen data.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker uses DoS attacks to disrupt healthcare services.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attacker destroys sensitive data to cause maximum impact.

Scenario.summary: The Outer Space campaign involves a complex attack flow that includes reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact phases.
